description: List ball information. By default, lists ball ID, radius, contact count, fixity, and position. Use keywords to list specific attributes, contacts, groups, properties, or results. Use model precision command to control floating point precision.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Ball getter methods: Ball.pos(), Ball.vel(), Ball.radius(), Ball.density(), etc. - requires iteration: for b in itasca.ball.list(): print(b.pos())

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: List ball attributes. If no keyword is given, then the available attributes are listed. Attributes are characteristics of balls such as mass and velocity. These are distinct from properties that represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions. Ball local damping coefficient. Ball density. Accumulated ball displacement vector as a result of cycling. The \(x\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling. The \(y\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling. The \(z\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling. Current orientation of Euler angles following the X,Y,Z convention (e.g., rotation about the \(x\) -axis followed by rotation about the y’ -axis followed by rotation about the z’‘ -axis) in degrees. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command). When active, the current ball orientation can be visualized. The \(x\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. The \(y\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. The \(z\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. Force applied to balls. The \(x\) -component of the force applied to balls. The \(y\) -component of the force applied to balls. The \(z\) -component of the force applied to balls. Sum of the contact force accumulated to the balls during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update. The \(x\) -component of the contact force. The \(y\) -component of the contact force. The \(z\) -component of the contact force. Fragment ID (see “ Fragment ”). Moment applied to balls. The \(x\) -component of the moment applied to balls. The \(y\) -component of the moment applied to balls. The \(z\) -component of the moment applied to balls. Sum of the contact moments accumulated to the balls during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update. The \(x\) -component of the contact moment. The \(y\) -component of the contact moment. The \(z\) -component of the contact moment. Location of ball centroid. Note that the model should be cleaned (see the model clean command) after this command is issued to ensure that potential contacts with neighboring pieces are updated. The \(x\) -component of the location of ball centroids. The \(y\) -component of the location of ball centroids. The \(z\) -component of the location of ball centroids. Ball radius. Current ball orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command). Ball angular velocity. The \(x\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians. The \(y\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians. The \(z\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians. Ball translational velocity vector. The \(x\) -component of the ball velocity. The \(y\) -component of the ball velocity. The \(z\) -component of the ball velocity.

keywords.name: contact
keywords.description: List the contacts with a ball. The contact ID, ID of the other contacting piece, contact type, activity state, inhibited state, and contact model are listed. By default, only active contacts are listed. Use the all keyword to list active and inactive contacts.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: List the extra variables at index i .

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: List the group names at slot s .

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: List ball properties. If no s is given, then the available properties are listed for each ball. If s is specified, then the value of that property is listed for each ball. Properties represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions. These are distinct from attributes that are characteristics of balls such as mass and velocity.

keywords.name: result
keywords.description: List the ball model results that have been tabulated. See the ball results command for further details.

examples.description: List default info (ID, radius, contacts, fixity, position) for all balls
List applied force for all balls
List value of extra variable at index 2 for all balls
List normal stiffness property for all balls

examples.command: ball list attribute velocity range group 'active'
examples.description: List velocity for balls in 'active' group

examples.command: ball list contact all range id 1 100
examples.description: List all contacts (active and inactive) for balls 1-100

keywords.name: attribute
keywords.description: List ball attributes. If no keyword is given, then the available attributes are listed. If a keyword is specified, then the value of that attribute is listed for each ball. Attributes are characteristics of balls such as mass and velocity. These are distinct from properties that represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions.

keywords.description: List the contacts with a ball. The contact ID, ID of the other contacting piece, contact type, activity state, inhibited state, and contact model are listed. By default, only active contacts are listed. Use the all keyword to list active and inactive contacts.
